package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for passthrough_wrapper_interface: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Wrapper structs that mostly forward one-to-one through an interface field with little added policy.
type CasePassthroughWrapperInterfaceDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CasePassthroughWrapperInterfaceModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CasePassthroughWrapperInterfaceRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CasePassthroughWrapperInterfaceModel
}

type CasePassthroughWrapperInterfaceService struct {
    repo *CasePassthroughWrapperInterfaceRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CasePassthroughWrapperInterfaceAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

type Worker interface {
    DoWork(input string) string
}

type workerImpl struct{}

func (w *workerImpl) DoWork(input string) string { return input }

type WorkerWrapper struct {
    inner Worker
}

func (w *WorkerWrapper) DoWork(input string) string {
    return w.inner.DoWork(input)
}

func PositivePassthroughWrapperInterface(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CasePassthroughWrapperInterfaceDTO) error {
    wrapper := &WorkerWrapper{inner: &workerImpl{}}
    result := wrapper.DoWork(input)
    _ = result
    return nil
}
